LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRAD EVANS, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF DENTPRO OF SALT LAKE INC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS MET ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING JURISDICTION. THE FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE APPLIES BECAUSE DEFENDANT ASSUMED THE FRANCHISE CONTRACT. SECOND, DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES SPECIFIC JURISDICTIONS. THE PARTIES HAD AN ONGOING RELATIONSHIP, DEFENDANT COMMUNICATED WITH PLAINTIFF REGARDING THE FRANCHISE AND REMITTED PAYMENTS TO PLAINTIFF. THE FRANCHISOR APPROVED OF THE ASSIGNMENT BASED UPON THE PARTIES CONDUCT SINCE THE ASSIGNMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO; SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET 2; TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET 2; AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS A DUPLICATE ENTRY.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS NOT CONTESTED. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SOUGHT CLARIFICATION ON ITS REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SUBMITTED ITS PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFF DENTPRO FRANCHISE CORP.'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION. SANCTION IN THE AMOUNT OF $2000.00 IS GRANTED, PAYABLE NO LATER THAN AUG. 24, 2012. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-04-2012 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Dept. 501 and was continued to today's calendar for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Sonia Wong #11288.

Joack Provine, Esq. for the Plaintiff: Dentpro Franchise Corp., a California Corp.
Plaintiff's Witness was placed under oath and direct examined.
No appearance by Defendants Brad Evans ind. and on behalf of Dentpro of Salt Lake, Inc.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff and against defendants for Judgment as follows:

Principal - $40,652.99
Interest - $26,132.80 for a Total of $66,785.79
Judgment was submitted and signed.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-20-2012 Failure To Post Jury Fees

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Aug-27-2012 Continued To Sep-04-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

Hearing For Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two; Special Interrogatories, Set Two; And To Compel Compliance With Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-13-2012 Off Calendar

Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interoggs
Off Calendar Aug-13-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dominic V. Signorotti, Esq. of Buchman Provine Brothers Smith LLP, Tel. 925.944.9700, for Plaintiff and moving party.
No other appearance.

The tentative ruling was not contested. However, Plaintiff's Counsel sought clarification on its request for sanctions. Plaintiff's counsel submitted its proposed order for the court's consideration.

After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: PLAINTIFF DENTPRO FRANCHISE CORP.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories is Granted. No opposition. Sanction in the amount of $2000.00 is granted, payable no later than Aug. 24, 2012. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BRAD EVANS, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF DENTPRO OF SALT LAKE INC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion to quash service of summons is denied. Plaintiff has met its burden of demonstrating jurisdiction. The Forum Selection Clause applies because defendant assumed the franchise contract. Second, defendant demonstrates specific jurisdictions. The parties had an ongoing relationship, defendant communicated with plaintiff regarding the franchise and remitted payments to plaintiff. The franchisor approved of the assignment based upon the parties conduct since the assignment. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES:

RULING: Argued and the Court vacated its tentative ruling and granted plaintiff's request to conduct jurisdictional discovery under the Mihlon case. The Court continued the matter to 07/11/11 at 9:30am for further hearing. Further opposition due 06/24/11 and further reply due 07/05/11. Parties to lodge courtesy copies with the department. Counsel may prepare a proposed form of order. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
